disclaimer: Educational assessment. CARSI is an IICRC-approved CEC provider; this is CARSI-original Australian training, not IICRC certification.
note: Australian-produced. Grounded in the course's own modules. Founder to review before wiring live.

quizzes.title: Introduction to Drying Hospitality and Lodging Sites — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: When reservations are unaffected by the water damage at a flooded hospitality venue, what does the course recommend doing with them?
options: Cancel every reservation until restoration is fully complete
Relocate them into authorised alternative networked properties using enterprise databases while rehabilitation timelines are assessed
Offer full refunds and stop accepting new bookings entirely
Move guests into the damaged wings under staff supervision

questions.questionText: To protect unaffected porous items such as linens, bedding, curtains and upholstery from cross-contamination during emergency mitigation, the course advises technicians to:
options: Leave them in place and run dehumidifiers around them
Discard all porous textiles as a precaution
Relocate them using manifest sealed polyethylene bags and bins to minimise unnecessary discard
Stack them in the corridor away from the water source
questions.sourceRef: M2 — Room and Content Considerations (Porous Textiles)

questions.questionText: How should room access control systems, telephone infrastructure and entertainment centres be handled after a flood?
options: Left powered on to keep their internal components warm and dry
Replaced immediately without any inspection
Wiped with damp microfibre cloths and returned straight to service
Security sealed and powered down until forensic component triage determines salvageability, avoiding short-out fire risks
questions.sourceRef: M2 — Room and Content Considerations (Electronic Systems)

questions.questionText: After disposing of flood-permeated kitchen foodstuffs, what does the course say must be done before appliances are reintroduced to service?
options: Validate the electrical grounding integrity of the appliances across the boards
Restart every appliance immediately to test it under full load
Reuse any sealed packaged goods that appear undamaged
Donate opened stock to reduce disposal costs
questions.sourceRef: M2 — Room and Content Considerations (Food Contamination Traces)

questions.questionText: In Structural Repair Staging, what is the purpose of dividing scaffold containment zones by floor levels and wings?
options: To reduce the number of heritage consultants required on site
To permit incremental guest reentry and minimise whole-asset closure durations through targeted compartmentalisation
To remove the need for scaffolding altogether
To accelerate the insurance payout process
questions.sourceRef: M3 — Structural Repair Staging (Levels and Wings)

questions.questionText: Why does the course advise erecting exterior structural scaffolding early to establish weatherproof facility shells?
options: To advertise the restoration works to passers-by
To provide outdoor storage for contaminated contents
To enable accelerated interior rehabilitation while protecting unaffected areas from storms
To satisfy the building's heritage colour requirements

questions.questionText: For a landmark hospitality property, how does the course say replacement millwork, period door hardware and crown moulding should be handled to match the original 1930s art deco design?
options: Use modern equivalents to keep reconstruction costs down
Substitute any available period-style materials without further checks
Remove the building's landmark status before works begin
Engage heritage architectural consultants to ensure the profiles precisely replicate the original designs in line with the facility's landmark status

questions.questionText: In Remediation Cleanup, what does the course require before containment barriers are removed to permit safe occupancy?
options: A single humidity reading taken at the doorway
Multivariate sensor testing across lead particulate dust, mould spore counts and VOC off-gassing to validate the zone meets indoor air quality thresholds
A visual inspection carried out by the front desk manager
Guest feedback surveys about how the air smells
